title: Defense Budget and Acquisition: Research & Analysis | CSIS
gist: This CSIS topic-landing page for Defense Budget and Acquisition is a directory of reports, commentaries, podcasts and events rather than a single article; its item descriptions flag munitions-inventory depletion after Operation Epic Fury, a drive to put the US industrial base on a wartime footing, expanding solid rocket motor production, drone supply-chain security, and acquisition reform.

insights.claim: A CSIS commentary states high expenditure of key munitions in Operation Epic Fury has created a window of vulnerability until US inventories return to pre-war levels and then reach the levels war planners desire.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Signals a multiyear munitions restocking cycle and a near-term US readiness gap; supportive of munitions-maker demand.
insights.quote: High expenditure of key munitions in Operation Epic Fury has created a window of vulnerability until inventories return to pre-war levels and then reach levels that war planners desire.
insights.locator: Rebuilding U.S. Missile Inventory: A Multiyear Project

insights.claim: CSIS frames a question of whether the US military nears the point of going 'Winchester' (running out of ammunition), tied to its 'Last Rounds?' report on missile inventory.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Reinforces a US munitions-shortfall narrative driving procurement urgency.
insights.quote: assess whether the U.S. military nears the point of going "Winchester"— or running out of ammunition.
insights.locator: U.S. Missile Inventory Check: A Window of Vulnerability?

insights.claim: CSIS describes accelerating production of solid rocket motors and strengthening the munitions industrial base as requiring an understanding of the market and the challenges and opportunities for expansion, given air and missile defense interceptors' critical role.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Solid rocket motor capacity is a flagged bottleneck and investment opportunity in the missile-defense supply chain.
insights.quote: Accelerating production of solid rocket motors and strengthening the munitions industrial base will require an understanding of the market and both the challenges and opportunities for expansion.
insights.locator: Solid Rocket Motors for Missile Defense

insights.claim: CSIS highlights the SHIPS Act and MAP as efforts to rebuild the US maritime industrial base (shipyards, mariners, logistics, commercial fleets) to bolster competitiveness against China, arguing sea power begins with economic power.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Maritime industrial policy is a structural spending theme; relevant to shipbuilding and logistics plays.
insights.quote: MAP and the SHIPS Act seek to rebuild the maritime industrial base—shipyards, mariners, logistics networks, and commercial fleets—that underpins U.S. naval strategy
insights.locator: Revisiting the Relationship Between Economic and Sea Power

insights.claim: CSIS points to an ongoing revolutionary overhaul of the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) and federal procurement reforms aimed at fielding emerging technologies at speed and scale.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Acquisition reform could lower barriers for non-traditional/defense-tech entrants; watch FAR overhaul outcomes.
insights.quote: the ongoing revolutionary Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) overhaul and federal procurement reforms
insights.locator: The FAR and Beyond: A New Era in Government Acquisition

themes.theme: Drones and next-generation warfare proving grounds

insights.claim: CSIS suggests Africa's open terrain could become a proving ground for next-generation warfare, helping the US and partners test drones, electronic warfare, and joint all-domain operations while signaling deterrence to China and Russia.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Identifies Africa as an emerging testbed for drone/EW/JADO capabilities, a niche for defense-tech experimentation.
insights.quote: Africa's open terrain could become the proving ground for next-generation warfare, helping the U.S. and partners test drones, electronic warfare, and joint all-domain operations while signaling deterrence to China and Russia.
insights.locator: Testing the Future of Joint All-Domain Operations in Africa

insights.claim: CSIS flags trusted drone manufacturing and resilient supply chains as central to securing US drone supremacy, via an Aerospace Security Project discussion.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Trusted-source drone supply chains are a policy priority; relevant to non-Chinese drone-component suppliers.
insights.quote: a discussion on trusted drone manufacturing, resilient supply chains, and the future of defense industrial cooperation.
insights.locator: Can Trusted Partners Help Secure U.S. Drone Supremacy?
